title: commicarpus decipiens
intro: Commicarpus decipiens is a species of plant in the family Nyctaginaceae, commonly known as the false desert holly or false desert broom. This shrub-like plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The leaves and stems of Commicarpus decipiens contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for wound healing and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Commicarpus decipiens, including teas and infusions, can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
